title: Euodia tietaensis
intro: Euodia tietaensis, also known as Tetradenia fruticosa, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Euodia tietaensis include limonoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Euodia tietaensis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or made into a tincture or capsule for the treatment of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Euodia tietaensis is classified within the family Rutaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. This family includes a diverse range of plants, including citrus fruits, rue, and various species of Euodia. Euodia tietaensis is also classified within the genus Euodia, which consists of a group of evergreen shrubs or small trees native to Asia. The genus Euodia is further divided into several species, with Euodia tietaensis being one of them. The classification of Euodia tietaensis into its family and genus is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other plants.
